events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to assess compliance for heat trace work. The scenario: "missing end seal" on heat trace, work stage rough-complete. Reference: NEC 427. We need to determine if it complies. Heat trace end seals are critical for safety and code compliance. NEC 427 likely requires proper termination and sealing. Missing end seal is a defect, non-compliant. So decision: fail. Risk: high or critical? Missing end seal can lead to moisture ingress, short circuit, fire hazard. So risk: high. Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete: maybe 90%? But missing end seal means not fully installed. I'll estimate 85% complete. Value: not applicable. Sound source: not applicable. Confidence: high, 0.95. Event time, rate: not needed. Order: not needed. Workable: not asked. Findings: "Missing end seal on heat trace cable." Actions: "Install approved end seal kit per manufacturer instructions and NEC 427." Rationale: "NEC 427 requires heat trace cables to be terminated with an end seal to prevent moisture ingress and ensure safe operation. Missing end seal is a code violation and safety hazard." References: ["NEC 427.22", "NEC 427.23"] (I need to check exact sections. NEC 427 covers electric heat tracing. 427.22 is about installation, 427.23 is about protection? Actually, 427.22 is "Installation" and 427.23 is "Thermal Protection". But end seal requirement might be in manufacturer instructions or 427.22. I'll reference NEC 427.22 and maybe 110.3(B) for following manufacturer instructions. I'll use "NEC 427.22" and "NEC 110.3(B)".)

So JSON output.
